items.title: Deal to end fighting would lead to Hormuz reopening, Iran says
items.summary: The deal which will pave the way for hostilities to end is close to being finalised, the US, Iran and mediators Pakistan say.

items.title: Switzerland to vote on plan to cap population at 10 million
items.summary: The right-wing Swiss People's Party calls the plan a "sustainability initiative",  but opponents say it is a recipe for chaos.

items.title: Married at First Sight Australia stars not told partners had drug and violence convictions
items.summary: Stars of the hit TV show say they did not know their on-screen husbands had previous convictions.

items.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX soars in stock market debut
items.summary: Musk is now worth $1.11tn according to the Bloomberg rich list, while SpaceX listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange with a value of $2.2tn.

items.title: Fans on concourses instead of in seats, claims Fifa
items.summary: Fifa says numerous empty seats were visible in the near-sell-out Group A match between South Korea and the Czech Republic because of fans remaining on concourses.

items.title: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in airstrike, Trump says
items.summary: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

items.title: Anthropic suspends new AI tools over US government security concerns
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, released publicly this week, sparked concerns about cybersecurity and hacking.

items.title: Warner Bros $111bn sale to Paramount approved by US justice department
items.summary: The approval marks a key development in the merger that will reshape media, allowing the continue of the takeover of the Hollywood studio, which owns CNN and HBO.

items.title: King leads tributes to 'giant of the art world' David Hockney
items.summary: The artist, who has died at the age of 88, "was one of life's true originals", King Charles says.

items.title: French town buries murdered child as questions mount over police failings
items.summary: The prime suspect in the killing of Lyhanna, 11, was reported to police nine months prior but never questioned.

items.title: More time needed for deadly Air India crash inquiry, officials say
items.summary: A statement says "significant progress" has been made into investigating the crash, in which 260 people died a year ago.

items.title: South Africa trolled by African fans in wake of World Cup loss
items.summary: The taunting reflects anger from parts of the continent over reports of xenophobia in South Africa.

items.title: Mother finds body of missing son two days after Kenya's Ebola quarantine centre protests
items.summary: Sylvester Muigai Ndung'u was in Nanyuki to fetch a school uniform on Tuesday, his mother says.

items.title: Discovery of €1.2m jewellery prompts fresh probe into former Spanish PM
items.summary: José Luis Rodriguez Zapatero is suspected of being unable to show proof he paid import duties on the jewellery.

items.title: David Hockney depicted a 'peaceful, gay paradise' when homosexuality was a crime
items.summary: The painter broke social taboos by celebrating same-sex relationships in his art.

items.title: The college scam that promised students fleeing war a new life in Finland
items.summary: BBC speaks to students from war-torn Myanmar who say they were cheated after paying to study abroad.

items.title: Why the economics make this the craziest World Cup ever
items.summary: From trade wars to soaring ticket prices, the 2026 World Cup is unlike any before it. Faisal Islam explores what this tournament reveals about our changing global economy.

items.title: What do Puerto Ricans think of the viral song about their homeland?
items.summary: People from Puerto Rico reflect on how the catchy tune has attracted attention to where they live.

items.title: 'I will come home safely': Indian sailor's last words to wife before a US strike killed him
items.summary: Patnala Suresh and his wife were looking forward to celebrating their 15th wedding anniversary this month.

items.title: David Hockney: Art's great innovator whose vivid paintings made him a household name
items.summary: The proud Yorkshireman, who also painted iconic pictures of America, was routinely described as Britain's greatest artist.

items.title: More than 50 Iranian military bases damaged in US strikes since start of war, satellite images show
items.summary: Bases across Iran have been hit by US strikes, with experts identifying damage to jets and warships.

items.title: The costs and challenges facing the 2026 World Cup
items.summary: The World Cup officially kicked off this week - but alongside the football, there are questions around heat, cost, the environmental impact and travel restrictions.

items.title: Niagara Falls: Is there a better spot to watch a World Cup game?
items.summary: Football fans gathered at a FIFA World Cup watch party at Niagara Falls for the Canadian and USA teams' opening matches.

items.title: Watch: Why is Trump not at the World Cup?
items.summary: All three leaders of the North America host nations have missed the tournament opening matches.

items.title: Watch: Four UFO videos newly declassified by US government
items.summary: The eyewitness footage shows bright orbs in the sky over an undisclosed location in the northeastern US.

items.title: Flip flop or deliberate? - Unpacking Trump’s strategy on Iran
items.summary: The BBC's Gary O'Donoghue looks at the US president's mixed messaging over the war and the questions it raises.
